raw_consumer_statement:
This is a follow-up to CFPB Case # XXXX ( closed prematurely ). 

I am requesting Citi re-review my dispute with XXXX  # XXXX ( {$970.00} on XX/XX/year> ) because key evidence and facts were overlooked. Citis latest letter ( XX/XX/year> ) states they will no longer respond, but I have unresolved issues that justify a refund. In all of Citis responses, their reason for not offering a refund was the merchants unwillingness to provide one.

Key Points Supporting a Refund : Misdiagnosis & Unnecessary Repairs : XXXX replaced all four brakes and rotors for nearly {$1000.00}, but the original issue remained entirely unresolved. XXXX diagnosed and fixed the real problem the next day, a failed wheel bearing and CV axle. 
Existing Warranty Coverage : My brakes were replaced shortly before this visit and were still rated 8 ( excellent ) at my state inspection. The parts were also under warranty at my trusted home garage, meaning I could have received a no-cost repair if they had been actually needed.

Failure to Return Parts : The shop promised to place the old brake pads/rotors in my car, which I requested for warranty purposes. They did not return them, removing my ability to verify condition or recover warranty value.

False Merchant Statement : The merchant told Citi that I never called back after their half-refund offer. I have a call log showing a 6-minute call with their district manager where that offer was discussed and declined. This is a factual inaccuracy in their response.

Incomplete Diagnostic Process : The merchant admitted after payment that they ran diagnostics only after completing the brake work, which revealed unrelated error codes they had not checked beforehand. I never received any details on any error codes and a check at my local garage when I returned home confirmed no codes were present.

Given these points, the shops actions caused unnecessary cost, failed to resolve the safety issue, and prevented recovery under my existing brake warranty. I request that Citis regulatory team re-evaluate this dispute with these facts considered.
